_comment: r4t harness config — copy to ~/.r4t/harnesses.json and edit. This file is the security boundary: it lives OUTSIDE the repo, and only tiers defined here can run. A roster Harness naming a tier absent from this file fails closed. Keys starting with _ are ignored everywhere.
_comment_placeholders: {prompt} is replaced with the built prompt as a single argv element — never through a shell. Defaults when a field is omitted: timeout_seconds=900, concurrency=1, max_turns_per_task=25, hop_limit=4, max_sends_per_turn=6.
_comment_throttle: throttle is team-wide, checked before any tier limit: max_concurrent caps live turns across ALL tiers; min_seconds_between_turn_starts spaces turn starts so a human can watch and intervene. active_ttl_rotations is how many idle passes an agent stays on the crash-recovery watch list after its last dispatch.

leader._comment: Orchestrator tier — Cursor agent CLI, generous limits.

junior-dev._comment: Cheap worker tier — OpenCode with whatever model the repo's opencode.json picks. invoke may also be a POOL (list of argvs) rotated round-robin per turn, e.g. across local models — agents never know which variant ran them.

pins._comment: agent name -> tier. A pinned agent's tier comes from HERE, silently overriding the roster's Harness line — an in-repo roster edit can't upgrade a pinned agent.
